laws.title: Public Law 36-256 | Commerce Dispersal Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: The act is an operative structural reorganization that transfers Commerce and Labor functions rather than expiring or being repealed.

provisions.evidence: The act may be cited as the "Commerce Dispersal Act" and includes severability.

provisions.text_summary: Dissolves Commerce and Labor and transfers its functions, enforcement, and related offices.

provisions.exact_change: Transfer Commerce and Labor functions to State or Justice as specified in subsections (a) through (j).
provisions.evidence: The source says the Department of Commerce and Labor "shall be rendered inoperable" and its functions "transferred."

provisions.text_summary: Transfers the Copyright Office and Copyright Royalty Board functions to the Department of State and the D.C. district court.
provisions.target: Library of Congress / Copyright Office / Copyright Royalty Board
provisions.exact_change: Move copyright administration out of the Library of Congress and into the Department of State, with CRB jurisdiction transferred to the D.C. district court.
provisions.evidence: The source says the Copyright Office "shall be transferred from the Library of Congress to the Department of State."

current_xml_comparison.detail: The branch has a subject-matter transfer note, but it still needs to be reduced to concise transfer history and cleaned of dump/Trello baggage.

final_recommended_action: Keep only a concise transfer note.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
Do not convert the restructuring into permanent positive-law sections.

provisions.text_summary: Amends Public Law 22-156 to place MPD under DOJ authority and revise leadership language.

provisions.evidence: Section 11 says the listed subsections of Public Law 22-156 "shall be amended to read."

laws.title: Public Law 41-271 | The Great Change Act of 2026
laws.chronology_conclusion: This broad integrating act repeals, amends, transfers, and preserves multiple prior laws while installing continuing reforms across security, commerce, ethics, military service, and whistleblower enforcement.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, table of contents, definitions, construction, severability, and effective date.

Repeals the Washington Diplomacy Complex Boundary Act, terminates obsolete commission authorities, strips anti-supersession clauses, preserves valid final actions, corrects clerical defects, blocks revival, and repeals the Oath and Offices Protection Act.
provisions.target: WDCBA; National Commission on Judicial Activity, Efficiency and Accountability Act; OOP
provisions.exact_change: Repeal or terminate the named prior laws and preserve only the stated savings / no-revival rules.

provisions.text_summary: Removes automatic security clearances, amends the National Security Act of 2026, bars automatic SCI/SAP access, creates expedited review, oversight, anti-retaliation, and classification safeguards.
provisions.exact_change: Amend the cited national-security statutes to eliminate automatic clearances/access and to add the new oversight and retaliation protections.

provisions.text_summary: Commerce Dispersal Act amendments and function transfers to State and Justice.
provisions.exact_change: Amend the Commerce Dispersal Act sections on commerce, trade, intellectual property, enforcement, and copyright functions as enacted.

provisions.text_summary: Amendments to 18 U.S.C. 205, definitions, protected government legal work, and savings clauses.
provisions.exact_change: Replace and supplement the 18 U.S.C. 205 text as enacted.

provisions.text_summary: Commissioned-officer minimums, temporary waivers, command authority, and validity of actions during noncompliance.
provisions.exact_change: Amend the affected staffing laws to the new minimum, waiver, command, and validity rules.

provisions.text_summary: Whistleblower-protection amendments, retaliation rules, burden of proof, identity protection, noncompliance rules, command responsibility, and reporting.
provisions.exact_change: Insert the new whistleblower, retaliation, remedies, identity, oversight, and command-responsibility provisions into the prior whistleblower framework.

provisions.text_summary: Conforming amendments and successor-reference rules.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The branch has the major 18 U.S.C. 205 rewrite present, but the title-18 note still needs to be collapsed to concise history; origin/main does not appear to carry the branch-only text.

final_recommended_action: Convert the direct amendments in Titles II-VI into actual text changes in the affected Code or prior-law sections.
Trim the note block down to a concise history note for the residual uncodified material.
Remove the Trello link and the full-text dump.
